name: Honolulu Coffee Experience Center
tagline: Their flagship roastery lets you taste the full range of Hawaiian coffee under one roof.
mustOrder: Kona peaberry pour-over — the good stuff
coffee flight — taste multiple Hawaiian estates side by side
parking: Ward Village parking structure, free with validation
talkStory: Founded in 1992, Honolulu Coffee is one of Hawaii’s largest specialty roasters. This Ward Village flagship is their mothership — not the same as the airport kiosks. They roast on-site with a Probat roaster you can watch in action, and coffee flights let you taste the difference between Kona, Waialua, and Ka’u beans side by side. They own their own farm in Kona for full seed-to-cup control. Pricey, but worth doing once.
